rationale: The citing paper references Du and Kamakura (2006) mainly as an example of prior HMM use in marketing and as a pointer that an S-state extension is possible. It does not rely on that paper for the core promotion-response model, estimation steps, hypothesis development, or empirical interpretation. The methodological foundation is instead built on standard HMM and Bayesian switching references (e.g., Rabiner, Kim and Nelson, Albert and Chib), so this is a background mention.
